playbook.intent: Multi-machine senior gets a new engagement → both machines productive on client stack within a half-day, no credential leakage between engagements.
playbook.scope: Half-day setup ceremony when a new outsource engagement starts. Output: laptop + desktop (+ optionally a client-issued VDI) productive on the client stack, with isolated credentials per engagement and dotfiles synced. Avoids credential mixing between concurrent engagements.

success-criteria.criterion: Laptop + desktop both build the client repo green

stage.name: Stack provision
stage.intent: Install the language + package manager the client demands.

tasks.task: Set up Python via Poetry if applicable
Verify on both laptop and desktop

stage.decision-gate: Advance only when both machines build the client hello-world.

stage.name: Credential isolation
stage.intent: Stop one client's keys leaking into another's repo.

tasks.task: Use trunk-based branching with branch-by-abstraction where applicable
Document onboarding rituals for new credential rotation
Decide VDI vs BYOD per VDI-vs-BYOD matrix

stage.decision-gate: Advance only when credentials cannot cross between engagements.

stage.name: Sync + bootstrap
stage.intent: Make the second machine identical to the first.

tasks.task: Document the bootstrap script for next engagement

stage.decision-gate: Done when both machines pass the same build + lint.

extra.value: [Choosing the stack — client mandate, Provisioning new hardware — uses what you own, VPN policy debate — work within what client allows]
['Client stack identified (language, package manager, IDE)', 'Credential vault tooling in place (1Password, Bitwarden, etc.)', Dotfiles repo exists]
[Using global credentials by accident — breaks isolation, Skipping dotfiles sync — second machine drifts in week 2, Treating VDI/BYOD as a personal preference — it's a security policy decision]
['Could I onboard a third machine in half the time using my doc?', 'Did I isolate every credential per engagement?', 'Did I match the client stack exactly, not approximately?']
{human: faion get-content new-project-machine-setup --format md, agent: faion get-content new-project-machine-setup --format context, json: faion get-content new-project-machine-setup --format json}
[{version: 0.1.0, date: 2026-05-20, note: 'Initial draft for F-064 P4 outsource-specialist persona, atomic angle'}]
